title: triticum orientale var. squarrosum
intro: Triticum orientale var. squarrosum, also known as a type of wild wheat or broomcorn millet, is a plant species that belongs to the family Poaceae. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are said to help soothe and calm the skin, reducing the risk of infection and promoting wound healing. The health benefits of Triticum orientale var. squarrosum include its potential to aid in digestion, lower cholesterol levels, and reduce blood pressure, making it a popular choice in herbal medicine. Triticum orientale var. squarrosum can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
